front: What are the four axioms (properties) that define a group (G, ∗)?
back:
1. Closure: For all a, b in G, a ∗ b is also in G.
2. Associativity: For all a, b, c in G, (a ∗ b) ∗ c = a ∗ (b ∗ c).
3. Identity: There exists an element e in G such that for every a in G, e ∗ a = a ∗ e = a.
4. Inverse: For every a in G, there exists an element b in G (denoted a⁻¹) such that a ∗ b = b ∗ a = e.

front: What is an Abelian (or commutative) group?
back: An Abelian group is a group (G, ∗) that also satisfies the commutative property: for all a, b in G, a ∗ b = b ∗ a. Not all groups are Abelian; those that are not are called non-Abelian groups.

front: Define the order of a group and the order of an element within a group.
back:
The order of a group (G, ∗) is the number of elements in G, denoted |G|.
The order of an element a in G is the smallest positive integer n such that aⁿ = e (the identity element). If no such n exists, the element has infinite order.

front: What is a subgroup? State the necessary and sufficient conditions for a subset H of a group G to be a subgroup.
back:
A subgroup is a subset H of a group G that itself forms a group under the same operation.
The subgroup test: A non-empty subset H of G is a subgroup if and only if for all a, b in H, the element a ∗ b⁻¹ is also in H.

front: What is a cyclic group? Define a generator of a cyclic group.
back:
A cyclic group is a group that can be generated by a single element. This means every element in the group can be written as a power (or multiple, if the operation is addition) of that single element.
A generator of a cyclic group G is an element g such that G = {gⁿ | n ∈ ℤ}. A cyclic group can have more than one generator.
